rationale: The citing paper explicitly uses Chevalier and Mayzlin (2006) as part of the core prior stream on online reviews and sales outcomes, not just as a background name-drop. It then contrasts its own approach against that stream by stating that, unlike those studies, it does not estimate the causal effect of average star ratings on sales and instead studies how information availability affects chain versus independent hotel performance. This citation therefore plays a meaningful framing and positioning role in the paper's stated contribution.
relation_description: The citing paper treats Chevalier and Mayzlin (2006) as a representative prior study on online review effects on sales, and then departs from that line by focusing on the information content of reviews and the declining value of chain affiliation in hotels.
